description: A vertical candid enforcement photograph (not a designed graphic) shot indoors in what appears to be a government office with carpeted floor and a black mesh office chair visible in the background. The frame is shot from behind and to the side, centered tightly on the moment of an arrest: a uniformed officer in a tan/khaki short-sleeve law-enforcement uniform (consistent with Florida Highway Patrol, referenced as @FHPOrlando in the tweet) stands at left, with a duty belt holding a black holstered item (likely OC/pepper spray or a taser) and a set of car keys with remote fobs dangling. The officer's hands are gripping the handcuffed wrists of a detained man whose back is to the camera; the man wears a dark patterned short-sleeve shirt printed with the words 'Hustle Hard' and floral/graphic designs, light blue denim jeans, and bright neon yellow-green sneakers. Silver metal handcuffs are clearly visible securing the man's wrists behind his back. There is no on-image text, no agency seal, no slogan, and no graphic-design treatment, color overlay, or typography — it is a raw documentary arrest snapshot. The propaganda function comes entirely from the accompanying tweet, which narrates the subject as a criminal 'illegal alien from Cuba' arrested at the USCIS Orlando office, framing routine ICE/state-partner enforcement as 'making America safe again.' Target audience: general public and immigration-enforcement supporters, intended to convey visible, tangible enforcement action.
notable_text: No on-image text. (Detainee's shirt reads 'Hustle Hard.')
